summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 6: re-detect against main (42 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (47 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 6 detection: 5 from clusters = 5 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 6 verify: 5 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Layout-component visibility predicates (canSee/visibleIf) are silently ignored at serialization — Section/Tab/Group authorization bypass
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/form/src/Form.php:178-199 (serializeSchema) and packages/form/src/Form.php:130-149 (flatten); compounded by packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:312-332 (resolveFormFields calls toArray with no record/no filter)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Thread the record through serialization and gate on visibility: have Form::toArray()/serializeSchema(?Model $record) skip any Component where !$item->isVisibleFor($record), and have Form::getFields()/flatten() likewise prune fields under a layout whose isVisibleFor($record) is false so effectiveFields() stops leaking them to FieldSchemaSerializer (render) and pruneUnauthorizedFields (write). InertiaDataBuilder::resolveFormFields must pass the current $record into toArray(). Alternatively, push layout canSee down onto the enclosed fields' canBeSeenBy at build time so the existing field-level enforcement covers them.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed, could not refute. Component::isVisibleFor() (Component.php:88-99) implements canSee+visibleIf and its docblock (lines 18-20) plus Form.php's docblock (lines 22-25) promise the controller evaluates it when serialising the payload. But grep across ALL non-test PHP source shows ZERO callers of Component::isVisibleFor() — it is dead code in production, unlike Column/Filter/Action/NavigationItem::isVisibleFor which are all wired into their serializers. serializeSchema (Form.php:182-196) spreads $item->toArray() and recurses unconditionally, never receiving a record. The deeper leak: effectiveFields() (Resources/Resource.php) -> Form::getFields() -> flatten() (Form.php:130-149) recurses through Component children UNCONDITIONALLY, so a field nested in Section->canSee(fn=>false) is still in the field list. That list feeds FieldSchemaSerializer (render, filters only per-field canBeSeenBy) and pruneUnauthorizedFields (write, checks only per-field canBeEditedBy) — neither knows about the enclosing layout. The JS FormRenderer.renderLayoutEntry (FormRenderer.tsx:84-136) has no visibility gate. Net: a field whose ONLY guard is an enclosing layout canSee()/visibleIf() leaks on render AND persist. Tests assert isVisibleFor() only in isolation (LayoutComponentsTest.php:96,142,150); none asserts serialize-time consultation, and it is not consulted. Classified framework-bug because the API/docblocks advertise these as authorization predicates ('false hides', evaluated 'when serialising') yet the framework silently ignores them — an author following the contract gets a silent bypass. Severity high not blocker: the canonical/documented auth path is field-level canSee/canEdit (API spec 05-api-php.md §2.1 line 291), which IS fully enforced on render and write; layout-level canSee is an undocumented-in-spec convenience; and the showcase baseline has zero layout canSee/visibleIf usage, so no live exposure today.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/form/src/Form.php:178-199 serializeSchema() emits EVERY layout entry unconditionally (it spreads $item->toArray() + recurses into getSchema()) and never calls Component::isVisibleFor(), nor does it receive the record. packages/form/src/Layout/Component.php:88-99 isVisibleFor(?Model) implements canSee + visibleIf and its docblock (lines 32-34, 'evaluated by the controller (CORE-006) when serialising the payload') promises it is consulted at serialize time. The consumer, packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:312-332 resolveFormFields(), calls $form->toArray() with NO record and applies NO filtering. Net effect: a Section/Tab/Group declared with ->canSee(fn($record)=>$user->isAdmin()) or ->visibleIf(...) is ALWAYS serialized and ALWAYS rendered by packages-js/ui/src/form/FormRenderer.tsx (renderLayoutEntry has no visibility gate). Contrast with field-level auth, which IS enforced: FieldSchemaSerializer.php:45 filters by canBeSeenBy and ResourceController::pruneUnauthorizedFields prunes writes by canBeEditedBy. So a field whose ONLY guard is an enclosing layout canSee() leaks both on render and (since no write-side layout check exists) on persist. The method is unit-tested in isolation (packages/form/tests/Unit/LayoutComponentsTest.php:139-150 'Component.canSee gates visibility') but NO test asserts it is consulted during Form::toArray()/serializeSchema, and it is not. This is the same Gate-vs-Policy class of authz-bypass swept elsewhere (versioning/realtime), but for form layout containers, still unwired.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Collab Yjs awareness channel authorization is keyed by FQCN but the REST snapshot endpoint broadcasts by Resource slug — live collab sync is dead in any slug-based app
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/realtime/src/Collab/AwarenessChannelAuthorizer.php:85 (resolveModelClass) in conjunction with packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php:94/121 (YjsUpdateReceived::dispatch($resource=slug)) and packages/realtime/src/Events/YjsUpdateReceived.php:44 (broadcastOn keys by raw modelType)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make AwarenessChannelAuthorizer::resolveModelClass also resolve via $registry->findBySlug($modelType) (mirroring CollabDocumentController::resolveModelClass), so the authorizer accepts the slug-keyed channel that the REST broadcast actually targets. (Equivalent alternative: have the controller normalize $resource slug -> FQCN before dispatching YjsUpdateReceived, and update the docs.) Either way both halves must share one channel-key convention, and an integration test must assert the dispatched YjsUpdateReceived channel equals an authorizable channel.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
Reproduced at code level against packages/realtime/src on current main. Confirmed chain: CollabDocumentController::store() persists model_type=$resource (slug, e.g. 'posts') and dispatches YjsUpdateReceived($resource=slug); YjsUpdateReceived::broadcastOn() builds channel arqel.collab.{slug}.{id}.{field} verbatim from modelType. The WS route arqel.collab.{modelType}.{modelId}.{field} (routes/channels.php) hands {modelType} to AwarenessChannelAuthorizer::authorize, whose resolveModelClass ONLY accepts a real FQCN (class_exists+is_subclass_of) or a Resource whose getModel()===$modelType — it never calls findBySlug/getSlug. The REST controller's own resolveModelClass DOES call findBySlug. So the two halves use incompatible model-key formats.

The candidate's exact deny-mechanism is slightly imprecise: the shipped docs (mcp-server/docs/guide/realtime-collab.md lines 80/93/163) prescribe modelType=FQCN ('App\Models\Post'), so a docs-conformant client subscribes to arqel.collab.App\Models\Post.{id}.body and IS authorized — but the REST broadcast lands on arqel.collab.posts.{id}.body (slug), which nobody listens to, so YjsUpdateReceived is never delivered. Conversely, if the app uses the slug as modelType to match the broadcast channel, the WS authorizer denies (no findBySlug). In BOTH plausible configurations the server-persisted snapshot replay event never reaches any subscriber, so live collab sync of YjsUpdateReceived is silently dead in a normal slug-based Arqel app. The candidate's net effect (collab sync never syncs, masked by tests) is correct.

Test masking confirmed: AwarenessChannelAuthorizerTest exercises FQCN (CollabAuthorizerFakePost::class) on the WS side and its single 'posts' case asserts denial only under an UNBOUND registry; CollabDocumentControllerTest persists model_type='posts' but never asserts the broadcast channel. No test pins the dispatched channel to an authorizable channel, so the cross-half divergence is invisible. This is a genuine framework bug in arqel-dev/realtime, not app misuse — the inconsistency lives entirely within the package's REST/WS/event triad and its docs. Severity high (advanced opt-in collab feature is dead end-to-end in any real app) rather than blocker (does not affect core CRUD; not yet wired into showcase UI).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
The REST collab endpoint and the WebSocket awareness channel disagree on the model-key format, so authorization always denies the very channel the REST writes broadcast to.

REST side (CollabDocumentController @ packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php): route is `/admin/{resource}/{id}/collab/{field}` where `{resource}` is a Resource SLUG (same convention as every other /admin/{resource} route). `resolveModelClass()` resolves the slug via `registry->findBySlug($resource)`. store() persists `model_type = $resource` (the raw slug, e.g. "posts") and dispatches `YjsUpdateReceived($resource, ...)` → broadcasts on channel `arqel.collab.posts.{id}.{field}`.

WS side (AwarenessChannelAuthorizer @ packages/realtime/src/Collab/AwarenessChannelAuthorizer.php, registered in routes/channels.php for `arqel.collab.{modelType}.{modelId}.{field}`): `resolveModelClass($modelType)` ONLY accepts a real FQCN (`class_exists && is_subclass_of(Model)`) OR a Resource whose `getModel()` === $modelType. It NEVER calls `findBySlug()`/`getSlug()`. So the slug "posts" resolves to null → authorize() returns false.

Live proof in apps/showcase (PostResource, slug 'posts' → App\Models\Post):
  REST  resolveModelClass('posts')  = 'App\Models\Post'   (works)
  WS    resolveModelClass('posts')  = NULL                (denies)
  WS    resolveModelClass('App\Models\Post') = 'App\Models\Post' (only FQCN works)

Net effect: in a normal Arqel app the REST endpoint writes/broadcasts under the slug, but no client can ever be authorized to subscribe to `arqel.collab.{slug}.*`, so YjsUpdateReceived is never delivered and real-time collaborative editing silently never syncs. Not caught by tests because the two halves are tested under incompatible conventions: CollabDocumentAuthorizationTest uses slug 'posts'; AwarenessChannelAuthorizerTest only uses FQCN (CollabAuthorizerFakePost::class), and its single 'posts' case asserts denial only for an UNBOUND registry, masking the bound-registry slug gap. Fix: make AwarenessChannelAuthorizer::resolveModelClass also resolve via findBySlug (mirroring the REST controller), OR have the REST controller key by FQCN.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: MCP resources/read double-wraps SKILL.md content: client receives a JSON-stringified envelope in the text field instead of raw markdown (and mimeType is dropped)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:381-389 (readResource re-wrap) + packages/mcp/src/McpServiceProvider.php:91-93 (fetcher returns full envelope) + packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:83-90 (registerResource has no mimeType param)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In McpServiceProvider, register the fetcher to return raw text: `static fn(string $u) => $skillResource->read($u)['contents'][0]['text']` — OR have readResource() detect/pass-through an already-enveloped fetcher return. Additionally, add a `?string $mimeType` parameter to registerResource() and thread `$entry['mimeType']` ('text/markdown' from SkillResource::list()) through it so the isset() branches in listResources()/readResource() become live. Also tighten McpConversationTest.php:209 from toContain to toBe (or assert the raw markdown without the JSON wrapper) so the masking is removed.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against the real McpServer + SkillResource (PHP run, not just static reading). McpServiceProvider.php:91-93 registers the resource fetcher as a closure returning $skillResource->read($uri), which is the COMPLETE MCP envelope ['contents'=>[['uri'=>,'mimeType'=>'text/markdown','text'=>$markdown]]]. McpServer::readResource() (McpServer.php:381-388) treats that return value as RAW content: it builds $entry=['uri'=>$uri,'text'=>stringifyResult($result)] and wraps it again in ['contents'=>[$entry]]. Because $result is an array (not a string), stringifyResult() (McpServer.php:435-441) JSON-encodes it. My repro output for arqel-skill://mcp gave text = `{"contents":[{"uri":"arqel-skill://mcp","mimeType":"text/markdown","text":"# SKILL.md fixture..."}]}` and json_decode(text) yields an array containing a 'contents' key — i.e. the markdown is double-wrapped and JSON-escaped. An MCP client (Claude Desktop) reading a SKILL.md gets an opaque escaped JSON blob, not usable markdown; the entire purpose of the resource is broken. Secondary defect confirmed: registerResource() (McpServer.php:83-90) accepts no mimeType param, so $res['mimeType'] is never set and the isset($res['mimeType']) branches at McpServer.php:358 (listResources) and :384 (readResource) are dead code; the 'text/markdown' mimeType that SkillResource::list() declares (SkillResource.php:72) is discarded at registration. My repro confirmed mimeType is absent from the resources/read response. The existing test masks the double-wrap: McpConversationTest.php:208-210 asserts ->toContain('# SKILL.md fixture for arqel-dev/mcp'), a substring that survives inside the JSON blob, instead of ->toBe. This is framework-bug not app-misuse: both the broken wiring (service provider) and the broken read handler (server) ship inside the arqel-dev/mcp package; no consumer code is involved. Severity high rather than blocker because the MCP server is a documented post-MVP initiative (PLANNING/13) and the defect is confined to the SKILL.md-resources feature without affecting the core admin framework; content is still technically recoverable via a client-side double-decode that no real client performs. Not a deferred/intended-behavior false positive (distinct from EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: SkillResource::read(string $uri) returns a complete MCP envelope: ['contents' => [['uri'=>..., 'mimeType'=>'text/markdown', 'text'=>$markdown]]] (packages/mcp/src/Resources/SkillResource.php:82-105). McpServiceProvider registers the resource fetcher as `static fn($uri) => $skillResource->read($uri)` (packages/mcp/src/McpServiceProvider.php:91-94), so the fetcher returns that whole envelope. But McpServer::readResource() (packages/mcp/src/McpServer.php:374-389) treats the fetcher's return value as RAW content and re-wraps it: `$entry = ['uri'=>$uri, 'text'=>$this->stringifyResult($result)]; return ['contents'=>[$entry]]`. stringifyResult() JSON-encodes the non-string array (McpServer.php:435-442). Reproduced end-to-end through the real McpServer + real SkillResource registration in apps/showcase: resources/read for arqel-skill://actions returns text = `{"contents":[{"uri":"arqel-skill://actions","mimeType":"text/markdown","text":"# SKILL.md — arqel-dev/actions..."}]}` — i.e. the markdown is double-wrapped and JSON-escaped (json_decode of the text yields an array with a 'contents' key). An MCP client (Claude Desktop) reading a SKILL.md therefore gets an opaque JSON blob, not usable markdown. Secondary: mimeType is never delivered — McpServer::registerResource() has no mimeType parameter (McpServer.php:83-90), so the mimeType:'text/markdown' that SkillResource::list() declares (SkillResource.php:72) is discarded at registration and the isset($res['mimeType']) branches in listResources()/readResource() are dead; verified both resources/list and resources/read responses omit mimeType. The existing test masks this because McpConversationTest.php:209 asserts `toContain('# SKILL.md fixture...')` (a substring that survives inside the JSON blob) instead of `toBe`. Fix: register the fetcher to return raw text (e.g. read(...)['contents'][0]['text']) or have readResource() pass envelope-shaped fetcher output through unchanged, plus thread mimeType through registerResource.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Sidebar navigation lists every Resource regardless of viewAny Policy — authorization-driven info disclosure
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In buildNavigation(), resolve the request user and skip any Resource whose viewAny is denied: pass $request->user() in, and before emitting an item gate it with the same logic ResourceController::authorize() uses — `if (Gate::has('viewAny') || Gate::getPolicyFor($model)) { if (Gate::forUser($user)->denies('viewAny', $model)) continue; }`. Mirror the controller's "no Policy = allow" rule so scaffold/Hello-World apps are unaffected. Optionally add a Resource::shouldRegisterNavigation(?Authenticatable):bool hook for explicit control. (Cleaner long-term: wire the already-built-but-unused arqel-dev/nav Navigation::build(?Authenticatable $user), whose NavigationItem::isVisibleFor(user) was specified for exactly this.)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning:
Reproduced at the code level and traced the full live path. HandleArqelInertiaRequests::buildNavigation() (lines 216-244) iterates $panel->getResources() and emits label/url/icon/group/sort for EVERY registered Resource with no user passed in and no Gate/Policy consultation. That payload becomes the panel.navigation shared Inertia prop, consumed by useNavigation() (packages-js/hooks/src/useNavigation.ts) and rendered by Sidebar.tsx. Meanwhile ResourceController::index authorizes 'viewAny' (ResourceController.php:51) and aborts 403 (authorize() at 354-365), proving the framework treats viewAny as a real per-resource authorization boundary. Net effect: a user denied viewAny by a Policy still sees the forbidden Resource's name/slug/URL/group/icon in the sidebar; clicking 403s, but the existence and naming of forbidden features leaks. Resource exposes getNavigationIcon/Group/Sort but no canViewAny()/shouldRegisterNavigation() hook, and no nav-build Policy check exists.

This is a genuine framework gap, not intended behavior: ADR-017 makes Laravel Policies the canonical authorization layer (policy-first), and the spec'd arqel-dev/nav package (NAV-001/002) defines Navigation::build(?Authenticatable $user) + NavigationItem::isVisibleFor(?Authenticatable $user) with acceptance criteria 'Visibility filtrada por user ao build' / 'Visibility condicional por user' — i.e. nav was explicitly designed to be user-filtered, but that package is unwired (no reference to it anywhere in packages/core/src) and the live middleware path implements none of it. This is distinct from the command-palette NavigationCommandProvider, which carries an EXPLICIT documented deferral ('we do not yet gate navigation by Policy ... deferred'); the main sidebar has no such acknowledgment and silently omits the check. Classified framework-bug because the middleware owns the nav build and an app cannot fix it without forking; an app cannot opt into per-resource nav authz with current public API.

Reproducibility caveat (why not blocker/high): the baseline apps/showcase ships ZERO Policies and zero viewAny gates (grep of app/ and Providers found none), so on the unmodified baseline nothing is forbidden and there is no live leak to demonstrate end-to-end. The disclosure only manifests in an app that registers a restrictive viewAny Policy — a latent gap that every policy-using app hits, but not an actively-firing exploit in the baseline. Impact is also bounded to feature metadata (labels/slugs/URLs/icons/groups), not record data: the controller still enforces viewAny and 403s on navigation, so no data is exposed. That confidentiality/least-privilege leak of forbidden feature names is real but limited, hence medium.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: HandleArqelInertiaRequests::buildNavigation() (packages/core/src/Http/Middleware/HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:216-245) iterates $panel->getResources() and emits a nav entry (label, url, icon, group) for EVERY registered Resource with NO user/Policy authorization check — no $user is even passed into the method (currentPanel()/buildNavigation() take no user, unlike userPayload/resolveAbilities). Panel::getResources() (Panel.php:311) returns the raw list unfiltered. Yet ResourceController::index authorizes 'viewAny' and aborts 403 on denial (ResourceController.php:51, 354-364 via Gate). Net effect: a user forbidden by Policy from a Resource still sees its sidebar link (label/slug/URL/group/icon) leaked via the shared panel.navigation Inertia prop consumed by useNavigation() + Sidebar.tsx; clicking 403s but the existence/name of forbidden features is disclosed. This is the LIVE nav path (HandleArqelInertiaRequests -> useNavigation -> Sidebar.tsx flat group payload), NOT the unwired arqel-dev/nav Navigation::build(). Resource exposes getNavigationIcon/Group/Sort but no shouldRegisterNavigation()/canViewAny() hook, and Policy is never consulted at build time. Distinct from the documented-deferred command-palette NavigationCommandProvider non-gating; this is the main sidebar.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: StateTransitionField::transitionTo shortName vs resolveTo regex divergence
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: StateTransitionField emits a `to` token (full short class name) that diverges from the write-path target token when a transition class has no static to() method
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In StateTransitionField::transitionTo(), replace the `return self::shortName($transition);` fallback with the same XxxToYyy class-name convention used by its siblings: apply `preg_match('/To([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9]*)$/', self::shortName($transition), $m)` and return `$m[1]` when matched (else the short name), so the emitted `to` token (e.g. `Paid`) slug-matches the write-path target — ideally extract a shared resolveTo() helper reused by HasWorkflow and TransitionAuthorizer to keep all three in sync.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely. Three sites derive a transition's target state; the field is the only one that diverges. StateTransitionField::transitionTo() (line 374) falls back to self::shortName($transition), emitting the FULL short class name (PendingToPaid) as the React prop `to`. HasWorkflow::resolveTransitionTo() (lines 365-373) and TransitionAuthorizer::resolveTo() (lines 204-210) instead apply /To([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9]*)$/ to extract the target segment (Paid). Verified the round-trip breaks: a standalone + a live Pest test (written, run green, then removed) show the field emits to:'PendingToPaid' which slugifyState()s to 'pending-to-paid', while the declared write-path target slugifies to 'paid' — assertTransitionAllowed()/transitionTargets() never match -> IllegalTransitionException. The framework's own CoverageGapsTest (tests/Unit/Coverage/CoverageGapsTest.php:142-149) asserts to === 'PendingToPaid', baking the broken token in as 'expected'. The React component (StateTransition.tsx:127-139,195-198) posts detail.to verbatim with no remap, and TransitionToEnforcementTest shows the intended transitionTo() argument is the state FQCN (PaidState::class), which DOES slug-match — proving the designed edge is state-to-state and the shortName fallback violates that invariant. Also internally inconsistent: `from` is a state key while `to` (no-to() path) is a transition-class name. Refutation attempts failed: no client-side remap, no normalization, and the two sibling resolvers exist precisely to derive the target via this regex — the field is the lone outlier. Note: SKILL.md claims WF-010 shipped an Http\Controllers\TransitionController write-path endpoint, but that file does not exist (provider comment still says it 'lands in WF-003+'), so today the break only manifests once an app wires the dispatched arqel:state-transition event to transitionTo()/the authorizer using the field's `to` token. Classified framework-bug (not app-misuse): the defect is the framework field emitting a token incompatible with the framework's own slug convention; an app following the documented class-name convention (transition without static to()) cannot make the advertised button actionable. Severity low: that convention path is optional/documented, the showcase baseline uses transitions([]) (free-form, unenforced) so it is latent, and apps declaring a static to() returning a state identifier are unaffected.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
Two different conventions derive a transition's target state, and they disagree for transitions lacking a static `to()`:

- StateTransitionField::transitionTo() (packages/workflow/src/Fields/StateTransitionField.php) falls back to `self::shortName($transition)` — the full short class name, e.g. `PendingToPaid` → emitted as `to: "PendingToPaid"` in the React props.
- HasWorkflow::resolveTransitionTo() and TransitionAuthorizer::resolveTo() (packages/workflow/src/Concerns/HasWorkflow.php:342, src/Authorization/TransitionAuthorizer.php:206) instead extract via regex `/To([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9]*)$/` → `PendingToPaid` → `Paid`.

So the UI renders a transition button whose `to` payload is `PendingToPaid`; when the client posts that back to transitionTo(), assertTransitionAllowed() compares `slugifyState('PendingToPaid')` (='pendingtopaid') against the declared transition's target `slugifyState('Paid')` (='paid') — they never match → IllegalTransitionException. The UI's advertised transition is un-actionable. Latent in the showcase because Ticket declares `->transitions([])` (free-form, no declared transition classes), so it is not exercised by the baseline; manifests only for apps with named transition classes that rely on the class-name convention instead of a static to() method. Lower confidence because it depends on that specific (documented but optional) convention path.
